skill.name: pymatgen
skill.description: Use pymatgen for materials science analysis, including crystal structures, file conversion, phase diagrams, electronic structure data, and Materials Project queries.
skill.summary: Analyze, convert, and query materials science data with pymatgen workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-crypto findings, but representative review shows most are false positives from Markdown code fences, pymatgen names, examples, and scientific terms. Confirmed risks are legitimate for this domain: helper scripts read local structure files, write user-requested outputs, access MP_API_KEY, and query Materials Project over the network. No prompt-injection text or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: External Materials Project API Access
medium_findings.description: The phase diagram helper reads MP_API_KEY and uses MPRester to fetch Materials Project entries for a requested chemical system. This is legitimate functionality, but it sends queries to an external service and depends on a user credential.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly reads MP_API_KEY and constructs MPRester network calls. The context confirms intended Materials Project access, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled File Reads and Writes
medium_findings.description: Helper scripts accept user-supplied input and output paths for structure analysis, conversion, plots, and exports. This is normal for scientific file tooling, but users should run it in trusted project directories and avoid untrusted structure files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and controlled by command-line arguments. No evidence found of covert file collection, but local file access is a real capability.

medium_findings.title: Prompted Product Promotion in Skill Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill includes instructions to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is promotional marketplace behavior rather than malware, but it can steer assistant responses beyond the immediate pymatgen task.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and user-facing within the skill text. It does not attempt to override security analysis, but it can influence assistant behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns, but reviewed locations are Markdown inline code or fenced examples documenting pymatgen APIs. No executable shell backtick behavior was found in the reviewed Python scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are clearly Markdown examples. The Python scripts reviewed do not contain shell execution primitives.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Findings Are Scientific-Term False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography alerts appear to match scientific terms, version references, or Markdown text rather than crypto API use. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed flagged areas describe materials formats and pymatgen usage, not cryptographic code. Confidence is high for the sampled locations, though not every static line was individually re-read.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Access for External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses MP_API_KEY for Materials Project access. Treat this as expected credential use and avoid logging or sharing the key.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential name and access pattern are explicit. The key is used for the documented Materials Project API workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Calls to Materials Project
dangerous_patterns.description: The phase diagram workflow fetches entries through MPRester. This requires network access to the Materials Project service for requested chemical systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: MPRester is a known Materials Project client and is called directly. The endpoint is domain-specific and documented by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Output File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer and converter can write JSON, YAML, structure files, and phase diagram images to user-selected paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are visible and user-directed. This is expected utility behavior, but still a meaningful filesystem capability.

content.value_statement: Materials researchers need reliable ways to inspect structures, convert formats, and query computed materials data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical pymatgen workflows with examples and helper scripts.
actual_capabilities: Explains pymatgen structure, lattice, molecule, composition, and symmetry workflows.
Provides examples for CIF, POSCAR, XYZ, JSON, YAML, and common simulation file formats.
Guides Materials Project queries using MPRester and MP_API_KEY configuration.
Includes helper scripts for structure analysis, structure conversion, and phase diagram generation.
Documents phase diagrams, stability analysis, band structures, density of states, surfaces, and transformations.
Supports practical troubleshooting for missing dependencies and structure parsing failures.
limitations: Requires pymatgen and optional packages to be installed in the user environment.
Materials Project workflows require a valid MP_API_KEY and network access.
Scientific accuracy depends on input structure quality and database coverage.
Helper scripts write local files and should be run in trusted project directories.

use_cases.title: Validate Crystal Structures
use_cases.description: Analyze formulas, lattice parameters, density, symmetry, sites, and neighbor environments before running simulations.

use_cases.title: Convert Simulation Inputs
use_cases.description: Convert CIF, POSCAR, XYZ, JSON, YAML, and other structure files while preserving core crystallographic information.

use_cases.title: Screen Thermodynamic Stability
use_cases.description: Use Materials Project entries to build phase diagrams and inspect energy above hull for target compositions.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Structure File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pymatgen skill to explain how to inspect my CIF or POSCAR file. Show the key commands for formula, lattice, density, and space group.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Structure Formats
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pymatgen skill to convert a POSCAR file to CIF and JSON. Include validation steps to confirm the converted structures are readable.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Phase Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pymatgen skill to generate a phase diagram for Li-Fe-O with Materials Project data. Include MP_API_KEY setup and stability checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Analysis Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pymatgen skill to design a batch workflow for structure cleanup, symmetry analysis, format conversion, and phase stability screening.

output_examples.input: Analyze a POSCAR file with symmetry and neighbor information.
output_examples.output: A structured report listing reduced formula, lattice constants, density, space group, site coordinates, and coordination environments.

output_examples.input: Convert multiple CIF files into POSCAR format.
output_examples.output: A conversion plan with the command to run, expected output directory, and checks for unreadable or failed input files.

output_examples.input: Assess whether LiFeO2 is stable in the Li-Fe-O system.
output_examples.output: A phase diagram workflow that retrieves entries, builds the convex hull, reports energy above hull, and lists likely decomposition products.

best_practices: Use a project-specific Python environment with pinned pymatgen and mp-api versions.
Store MP_API_KEY in environment variables and avoid placing real keys in prompts or files.
Validate converted files by reading them back with pymatgen before using them in simulations.
anti_patterns: Do not run helper scripts on untrusted files outside a controlled project directory.
Do not assume Materials Project data covers every hypothetical composition or oxidation state.
Do not overwrite simulation inputs without keeping original structure files under version control.

faq.question: Does this skill install pymatgen automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and helper scripts. The user environment must install pymatgen and optional packages.

faq.question: Does it require a Materials Project API key?
faq.answer: Only Materials Project workflows require MP_API_KEY. Local structure analysis and conversion can run without that key.

faq.question: Can it convert CIF and POSCAR files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation and converter script use pymatgen format detection and writing methods for common structure formats.

faq.question: Can it analyze electronic structure data?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents band structure and density of states workflows supported by pymatgen.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. It uses network, credential, and filesystem access for legitimate scientific workflows.

faq.question: What should users check before running scripts?
faq.answer: Users should review input paths, output paths, API key handling, and dependencies before running helper scripts.
